scope_boundary: deterministic generation-mode and resource-budget accounting discipline only; no route-search benchmark, hidden-cost audit, compression utility measurement, useful-solution-per-second result, routing-quality result, deployment trace, model-quality result, or runtime behavior result
transition_reason: The repository now has deterministic generation-mode and resource-budget fixture accounting that helps keep efficiency claims attached to baseline, negative-control, quality, residual, fallback, and no-promotion fields. The Efficient ASI chapter already frames efficiency as routed, compressed, reused, governed, and tested cognition with visible residuals. These artifacts improve the accounting discipline around that design claim, but they do not run route search, measure cost-quality tradeoffs, audit hidden human repair burden, reproduce compression or routing benchmarks, or establish useful-solution-per-second performance.
required_artifacts: route-search workload suite with task contracts, quality predicates, and authority ceilings
matched baseline route records including rejected lower-cost routes
cost, quality, residual, verification, and fallback measurements
hidden human repair burden and deferred-work audit
compression or reuse utility benchmark with negative controls
independent review of source interpretation adequacy
accepted upward evidence-transition review for any narrowed stronger claim
negative_results: The Efficient ASI Hypothesis remains at argument
generation-mode and resource-budget fixtures are deterministic accounting fixtures
no route-search workload benchmark was run
no hidden-cost or human-repair audit was run
no useful-solution-per-second efficiency claim is established
downgrade_triggers: chapter text interpreted as measured route efficiency
latency-only proxy used as useful-solution-per-second evidence
promotion_burden: Move above argument only after the claim is narrowed to deterministic accounting behavior that passed, or after route-search workloads, matched baselines, negative controls, cost-quality-residual measurements, hidden-cost audits, fallback traces, and independent source-interpretation review are recorded with accepted evidence-transition records.
limitations: deterministic fixtures do not run a routed ASI stack
schema validation does not prove route quality or compression utility
finite proof envelopes do not establish measured efficiency
useful-solution-per-second remains a target metric, not a measured local result
no independent model-evaluation or source-interpretation review has been recorded
reviewer_independence: local maintainer-agent review only; not an independent benchmarking, model-evaluation, economic, or source-interpretation audit
acceptance_blockers: no hidden human repair burden audit
non_claims: does not promote Efficient ASI above argument
does not prove compression or reuse utility
does not prove runtime routing or deployment behavior
